article_title: Apple's Smart Glasses Take Shape With Four Frame Styles and a 2027 Launch Window
summary: Submission content is reviewable, but the PR branch includes unrelated submissions and cannot be merged as-is

findings.message: PR branch contains unrelated submission files
findings.details: PR #957 is titled and branched for the Apple smart-glasses submission, but it also includes src/content/submissions/2026-04/2026-04-19T19-16-45Z_rust-195-adds-cfg_select-extends-if-let-guards-and.json and src/content/submissions/2026-04/2026-04-19T19-16-40Z_india-sets-up-aigeg-to-centralize-ai-governance-an.json. Those belong to other review flows, and the India PR (#958) has already been merged. Merging #957 as-is would create a contaminated merge.

content_preview.title: Apple's Smart Glasses Take Shape With Four Frame Styles and a 2027 Launch Window
content_preview.summary: Apple is reportedly testing four frame styles for its first smart glasses, signaling a more consumer-friendly wearables push ahead of a possible 2027 launch.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Apple is reportedly testing at least four frame styles for its first smart glasses, including a large rectangular frame, a slimmer rectangular option, and two oval variants, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/12/apple-reportedly-testing-four-designs-for-upcoming-smart-glasses/) and [The Next Web](https://thenextweb.com/news/apple-smart-glasses-four-frame-designs-testing-2027/). The same reporting says Apple is also evaluating colors such as black, ocean blue, a...

recommendations: Clean the PR branch so it contains only the intended Apple smart-glasses submission before resubmitting for merge

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is clear and proportionate. It stays within rumor-reporting boundaries and presents the hardware direction as contingent rather than confirmed product fact.
editor_notes.source_verification: I read all three listed sources directly. TechCrunch supports the four frame styles, 2027 sales target, possible end-of-year unveiling, and display-free first-generation framing. TechRadar supports Greg Joswiak's 'some inevitability' comment and the linkage to broader smart-glasses roadmap hints. The Next Web supports the color options, N401 chip reporting, two-camera setup, and late-2026 production plus 2027 launch window claims.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: For the intended article, the claims align with the listed sources and the rumor status is signposted appropriately.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The piece does not treat unannounced hardware as settled fact.
editor_notes.originality: Originality check passed for this Apple frame-design and launch-window angle.
concerns: The PR is structurally invalid because it contains unrelated submission files in addition to the intended article.
One of the unrelated files corresponds to India AIGEG, whose own PR (#958) has already merged, so merging this branch would produce a polluted history.
recommendations: Remove the unrelated Rust and India submission files from PR #957 and keep only the Apple smart-glasses submission.
Resubmit or update the PR branch, then request re-review.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Request changes. The article itself is reviewable, but the PR cannot be merged until the branch is cleaned up.
